response.justification: The candidate has genuine geospatial experience (GISCollective, Green Map web GIS) and real low-latency systems work (DLang microservices handling millions of requests per second at Sociomantic), which maps loosely onto the role's data-pipeline and performance demands. However, modern C++ experience is thin and dated (a brief stint at National Instruments), there is no Rust, and no direct routing or navigation-guidance work. The profile skews toward full-stack web and D-language tooling rather than production C++ geospatial pipelines, making the fit plausible but uncertain.
strengths: Hands-on geospatial/GIS platform development (GISCollective, Green Map)
Low-latency, high-throughput distributed systems experience with async I/O and caching
Strong engineering craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, testing tools authorship
concerns: Minimal and outdated modern C++ experience; no Rust
No routing, navigation guidance, or map-tiling pipeline background
Recent work is TypeScript/Node full-stack, far from the role's core stack
